In polar coordinates (r, θ) the limits  
1 ≤ r ≤ 2 and −π/2 ≤ θ ≤ π/2  

describe the part of the annulus 1 ≤ r ≤ 2 that lies between the downward and upward rays θ = −π/2 and θ = π/2.  
That is the right–hand half of the annulus between the circles of radius 1 and 2 (a “half-ring”).

Integral to evaluate  
x = r cos θ, dA = r dr dθ.

∬_R x dA  
= ∫_{θ=−π/2}^{π/2} ∫_{r=1}^{2} (r cos θ)(r) dr dθ

(7/3)·2 = 14/3.

Question: Find the area enclosed inside the cardioid $$r = 3 - 3 \text{sin} \theta$$ and outside the cardioid $$r = 1 + \text{sin} \theta .$$
o3 Answer:
Let  

 outer cardioid : r₁ = 3 − 3 sin θ  
 inner cardioid : r₂ = 1 + sin θ  

For a point (r, θ) to be 

• inside r₁ : 0 ≤ r ≤ r₁(θ)  
• outside r₂ : r ≥ r₂(θ)

hence we must have r₂(θ) ≤ r ≤ r₁(θ) and therefore r₂(θ) ≤ r₁(θ).

------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Intersection angles

r₁ = r₂ ⇒ 3 − 3 sin θ = 1 + sin θ  
     ⇒ 2 − 4 sin θ = 0  
     ⇒ sin θ = ½.

Thus the two cardioids meet when θ = π/6 and θ = 5π/6 (and again every 2π).

------------------------------------------------------------------
2. Angles where the outer cardioid is outside the inner one

r₁ ≥ r₂ ⇔ sin θ ≤ ½.  
On the interval 0 ≤ θ < 2π this holds for

 I = [0, π/6] ∪ [5π/6, 2π].

Only over these angles can the desired region exist.

------------------------------------------------------------------
3. Polar‐area integral

Area = ½ ∫₍ᴵ₎ (r₁² − r₂²) dθ.

Compute the integrand:

r₁² − r₂² = (3 − 3 sin θ)² − (1 + sin θ)²  
      = 9 − 18 sin θ + 9 sin²θ − 1 − 2 sin θ − sin²θ  
      = 8 − 20 sin θ + 8 sin²θ.

Hence

Area = ½ ∫₍ᴵ₎ (8 − 20 sin θ + 8 sin²θ) dθ  
   = ∫₍ᴵ₎ (4 − 10 sin θ + 4 sin²θ) dθ.

Replace sin²θ by (1 − cos 2θ)/2:

Integrand = 4 − 10 sin θ + 4·(1 − cos 2θ)/2  
     = 6 − 10 sin θ − 2 cos 2θ.

Antiderivative F(θ):

F(θ) = 6θ + 10 cos θ − sin 2θ.

------------------------------------------------------------------
4. Evaluate on the two sub‐intervals

(a) 0 → π/6  

F(π/6) = π + 5√3 − √3/2 = π + 9√3/2  
F(0)    = 10  

Contribution:  (π + 9√3/2) − 10.

(b) 5π/6 → 2π  

F(2π)   = 12π + 10  
F(5π/6) = 5π − 5√3 + √3/2 = 5π − 9√3/2  

Contribution: (12π + 10) − (5π − 9√3/2)
            = 7π + 10 + 9√3/2.

------------------------------------------------------------------
5. Total area

Add the two contributions:

Area = [π + 9√3/2 − 10] + [7π + 10 + 9√3/2]  
      = 8π + 9√3.

------------------------------------------------------------------
Result

The area enclosed by the cardioid r = 3 − 3 sin θ and outside the cardioid r = 1 + sin θ is

  A = 8π + 9√3.
